Notably, Samyuktha has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Samyuktha competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, a doubles best of #4, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: ORC top-4 finisher.

  • Surya Peechara — 3 events in XD, best: #2

Samyuktha has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Samyuktha has been seeded #1 or #2 in 5 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Samyuktha's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

seth_li.name: Seth Li
seth_li.summary:
Representing New Jersey, Seth Li has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U13 BD, U13 BS, U13 XD, U15 BD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 15 career titles across 34 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Seth is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (3 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Seth competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 2 singles titles, 9 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U13 BD, U13 BS, U13 XD, U15 BD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (BD U13) • 5 ORC titles.

The statistics paint a vivid picture — Seth boasts 45 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a career +728 point differential, a biggest win margin of 34 points.

Seth has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 13 different players:
  • Kimaya Krishna — 18 events, 1 title together in U13/XD
  • Rithik Rasamsetti — 14 events in BD/U13, producing 6 titles together
  • Terence Cai — 9 events in BD/U15, producing 2 titles together

A veteran of the junior circuit, Seth has competed across U11, U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Seth has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Seth has been seeded #1 or #2 in 6 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

With 6 seasons of competitive experience and 15 titles, Seth is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

siddhi_thorat.name: Siddhi Thorat
siddhi_thorat.summary:
Representing California, Siddhi Thorat is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-4 finish at Selection places Siddhi in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Notably, Siddhi has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Siddhi is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, a doubles best of #2, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-4 (U13 GD) • Junior Nationals top-3 (GD U11, XD U11) • 3 ORC titles.

The statistics paint a vivid picture — Siddhi boasts a solid 62% win rate over 93 matches, a career +511 point differential, a biggest win margin of 36 points.

With 19 different doubles partners across the career, Siddhi adapts to anyone:
  • Chiranjeev Deepak — a trusted U13/XD partner (8 events, best: #2)
  • Michael Sun — 6 events in XD, producing 2 titles together
  • Rachel Yao — a trusted GD/U13 partner (5 events, best: #4)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 6 podium finishes across 5 tournaments. Siddhi is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Siddhi has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Siddhi has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Siddhi has been seeded #1 or #2 in 5 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

Over 5 seasons and 3 titles, Siddhi has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.
